message:

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.5","title":"Tests: wait-for/quiescence helpers (unit+integration)","description":"# Task: Tests for wait-for/quiescence helpers

## Goal
Prevent regressions in the timing primitives.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for:
  - backoff schedule behavior
  - timeout error contents
- Integration tests for:
  - quiescence with synthetic producer/consumer

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail with actionable output when timing primitives regress.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.6","title":"E2E: flake guard mode (repeat-run a representative case)","description":"# Task: E2E flake guard mode

## Goal
Add an optional CI mode (or local mode) that reruns one representative E2E case multiple times to detect timing flakiness early.

## Requirements
- Implement a runner flag (or separate script mode) to run a chosen case N times.
- Ensure artifacts are retained on first failure with:
  - per-iteration logs
  - summary of timings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Flake guard mode can catch timing regressions without making normal CI too slow.

 {"id":"wa-upg.3.7","title":"Docs: timing determinism guidelines (no sleeps; quiescence patterns)","description":"# Task: Document timing determinism guidelines

## Goal
Prevent flakiness from re-entering the codebase by documenting best practices.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - when to use wait-for vs quiescence
  - how to set timeouts and backoff
  - how to write actionable timeout errors
  - explicit "no fixed sleep" rule + exceptions policy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Contributors can follow a short checklist to write deterministic tests.

Commit bc642c7.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:30:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-upg.4","title":"[EPIC] Stable pane identity handshake (pane_uuid across churn)","description":"# [EPIC] Stable pane identity handshake (`pane_uuid`)

## Mission
Give every pane a **stable identity** (`pane_uuid`) that survives common churn:
- renames
- title changes
- tab reparenting / workspace moves
- session recording and replay
- (eventually) distributed mode

This unlocks higher-trust automation:
- fewer "sent to wrong pane" failures
- better correlation across timelines
- durable per-pane histories

## Why This Matters
WezTerm `pane_id` is useful but not sufficient as a long-lived identity:
- IDs can be reused across sessions
- names and titles change
- in distributed mode we need a stable cross-machine identifier

A stable `pane_uuid` lets the system reason about "this is the same pane" even when superficial attributes change.

## Design Constraints
- Observation loop should be **pure read** when possible.
- If we must write to establish identity, it must be:
  - explicit, minimal, and audited
  - safe and reversible
  - never continuous spam

## Candidate Approaches (evaluate)
1) WezTerm user vars (preferred if list/get-text surfaces them)
2) OSC marker handshake (write-once marker captured by watcher)
3) Pure fingerprint (fallback): derive a probabilistic identity from stable features (domain + cwd + creation time)

## Success Criteria
- For a pane that is renamed and moved, wa continues to associate output/events with the same stable identity.
- Identity assignment is safe-by-default and does not violate observe/act separation without explicit justification.

## Testing
- Unit tests for identity parsing/derivation.
- Integration tests with synthetic pane registry updates.
- E2E:
  - create pane, assign `pane_uuid`, rename/move, verify `pane_uuid` stable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A clear identity strategy is selected and implemented.
- `pane_uuid` is stored in DB and exposed via robot/CLI.
- Tests and at least one E2E scenario validate stability.

## Mission
Give every pane a **stable identity** (`pane_uuid`) that survives common churn:
- renames
- title changes
- tab reparenting / workspace moves
- session recording and replay
- (eventually) distributed mode

This unlocks higher-trust automation:
- fewer "sent to wrong pane" failures
- better correlation across timelines
- durable per-pane histories

## Why This Matters
WezTerm `pane_id` is useful but not sufficient as a long-lived identity:
- IDs can be reused across sessions
- names and titles change
- in distributed mode we need a stable cross-machine identifier

A stable `pane_uuid` lets the system reason about "this is the same pane" even when superficial attributes change.

## Design Constraints
- Observation loop should be **pure read** when possible.
- If we must write to establish identity, it must be:
  - explicit, minimal, and audited
  - safe and reversible
  - never continuous spam

## Candidate Approaches (evaluate)
1) WezTerm user vars (preferred if list/get-text surfaces them)
2) OSC marker handshake (write-once marker captured by watcher)
3) Pure fingerprint (fallback): derive a probabilistic identity from stable features (domain + cwd + creation time)

## Success Criteria
- For a pane that is renamed and moved, wa continues to associate output/events with the same stable identity.
- Identity assignment is safe-by-default and does not violate observe/act separation without explicit justification.

## Testing
- Unit tests for identity parsing/derivation.
- Integration tests with synthetic pane registry updates.
- E2E:
  - create pane, assign `pane_uuid`, rename/move, verify `pane_uuid` stable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A clear identity strategy is selected and implemented.
- `pane_uuid` is stored in DB and exposed via robot/CLI.
- Tests and at least one E2E scenario validate stability.

 {"id":"wa-upg.4.1","title":"Research: best pane_uuid strategy with WezTerm (user vars/OSC/fingerprint)","description":"# Task: Research pane_uuid strategy

## Goal
Determine the best, safest mechanism to assign and read a stable `pane_uuid`.

## Questions to answer
- Does `wezterm cli list --format json` expose user vars or any stable per-pane metadata?
- Can wa set a per-pane user var without sending visible text?
- Can we use an OSC sequence that:
  - is not visible to the user
  - can be observed by the watcher
  - is safe and idempotent
- If none of the above, what is the best fingerprint fallback and what are the collision risks?

## Deliverable
- A written decision (in this bead) selecting:
  - primary approach
  - fallback approach
  - any required opt-in flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- The chosen approach is feasible with WezTerm primitives and aligns with observe/act safety constraints.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:39:40.327746299Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:31:44.118227734Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:31:44.118112177Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-wezterm","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.4.2","title":"Spec: pane_uuid lifecycle, storage schema, and invariants","description":"# Task: Spec pane_uuid lifecycle + schema

## Goal
Specify how `pane_uuid` is created, stored, and used across the system.

## Requirements
- Lifecycle:
  - when assigned
  - when re-assigned (if ever)
  - how to handle panes that cannot be assigned (fallback)
- Storage:
  - store `pane_uuid` in panes table
  - store historical mapping of `pane_id` → `pane_uuid` (optional) for auditing
- Invariants:
  - within a workspace, `pane_uuid` is unique
  - identity assignment is idempotent
  - no silent reassignment that could lead to "wrong pane" actions
- Exposure:
  - robot state includes `pane_uuid`
  - CLI shows `pane_uuid` in verbose modes

## Testing
- Define fixture expectations for:
  - rename
  - move
  - pane disappearance and reappearance

## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec clearly defines success/failure behavior and the safety posture.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:39:51.568740781Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:31:46.393291299Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:31:46.393198495Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-storage","area-wezterm","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.4.3","title":"Implement pane_uuid assignment + mapping in pane registry","description":"# Task: Implement pane_uuid assignment + mapping

## Goal
Implement the chosen `pane_uuid` strategy in the pane registry/runtime.

## Requirements
- Assign or discover `pane_uuid` for each observed pane.
- Ensure assignment is:
  - idempotent
  - bounded (no repeated writes)
  - safe (no user-visible spam)
  - auditable (record when/why assignment was performed)
- Update pane registry to key stable identity by `pane_uuid` and treat `pane_id` as a session-local handle.

## Testing
- Unit tests for parsing/decoding markers or user vars.
- Integration tests for registry stability across rename/move events.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The watcher can maintain stable identity across common pane churn.
- Identity assignment never runs in a tight loop or blocks the ingest loop.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:40:02.523993784Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:21:11.012760484Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:21:11.012667489Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-ingest","area-tests","area-wezterm","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.4.4","title":"Persist pane_uuid in DB and expose via robot/CLI","description":"# Task: Persist and expose pane_uuid

## Goal
Persist stable pane identity and expose it so all layers can use it.

## Requirements
- DB schema:
  - add `pane_uuid` to panes table (or equivalent)
  - migrations and upgrade tests
- Query surfaces:
  - `wa robot state` includes `pane_uuid`
  - human CLI includes `pane_uuid` in verbose pane listings
- Backwards behavior:
  - if pane_uuid missing/unavailable, present explicit "unassigned" status (do not fake certainty)

## Testing
- Migration tests: schema version upgrade preserves existing data.
- Output schema tests: robot output includes stable fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pane_uuid is durable, queryable, and visible.

 {"id":"wa-upg.4.5","title":"Tests: pane_uuid stability across rename/move/session churn","description":"# Task: Tests for pane_uuid stability

## Goal
Ensure stable identity remains stable across realistic churn.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - marker/user-var parsing is robust
  - fingerprint fallback collision behavior is understood and bounded
- Integration tests:
  - registry maintains stable identity across:
    - title changes
    - cwd changes
    - tab moves
    - pane disappearance/reappearance

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover the churn cases that historically cause "wrong pane" actions.

## Goal
Prove in a real WezTerm session that pane_uuid is stable across common churn.

## Scenario
- Create a pane.
- Ensure pane_uuid assigned.
- Rename pane/title and/or move between tabs.
- Verify:
  - same pane_uuid continues
  - wa continues to attribute output/events correctly

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - initial pane state
  - post-churn pane state
  - logs showing identity continuity

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E deterministically validates identity stability.

 {"id":"wa-upg.4.7","title":"Docs: pane_uuid identity model (assignment, stability, debugging)","description":"# Task: Document `pane_uuid`

## Goal
Document the stable pane identity model so humans and agents can:
- understand how `pane_uuid` is assigned
- know what invariants are guaranteed
- debug identity mismatches safely

## Content to include
- Rationale: why `pane_id` is not sufficient as a long-lived identity.
- Strategy chosen:
  - user vars vs OSC handshake vs fingerprint fallback
  - when we write to a pane (if ever), and how we avoid "spam" writes
- Lifecycle:
  - when `pane_uuid` is created
  - how it persists across rename/move
  - what happens on pane recreation
- Storage model:
  - where `pane_uuid` lives in DB
  - how robot/CLI surfaces it
- Debugging guide:
  - how to confirm a pane's identity
  - what evidence is used when identity is uncertain
  - safe remediation steps

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future contributor can reason about pane identity without consulting PLAN.md.
- Docs are explicit about observe/act separation and any write-once handshakes.

 {"id":"wa-upg.5","title":"[EPIC] Storage/indexing perf hardening (scale, durability, observability)","description":"# [EPIC] Storage/indexing performance hardening

## Mission
Make storage and indexing robust and fast at scale:
- sustained ingest without lag spikes
- fast FTS queries on large corpora
- predictable compaction/checkpoint behavior
- observable performance (metrics + budgets)

## Why This Matters
wa's DB is its "world memory". If storage is slow or flaky:
- automation becomes laggy
- searches feel useless
- users lose trust

## Scope
This epic is about **hardening** beyond initial correctness:
- indexing progress tracking
- write amplification reduction (batching)
- WAL checkpoint strategy
- FTS incremental sync and vacuum/optimize cadence
- hot-path profiling and budgets

## Success Criteria
- Under stress (large transcripts, many panes), wa stays responsive.
- FTS queries remain fast with stable p95 latency.
- Storage health surfaces make it obvious when indexing is behind.

## Testing
- Benchmarks with budgets for:
  - ingest append
  - FTS query common cases
  - incremental indexing
- Stress E2E:
  - generate large transcript and validate search + no runaway memory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Performance hardening tasks are implemented with measurable improvements.
- Budgets are enforced in CI and regressions are detectable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:40:46.641727420Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:55:14.839277732Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T21:55:14.839149544Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-perf","area-storage","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.5","depends_on_id":"wa-upg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":215,"issue_id":"wa-upg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ABSORBED: Now includes wa-3g9.4 (Incremental FTS sync) which was reparented here since FTS indexing performance belongs under storage/indexing hardening, not general performance optimizations.","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:34:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.5.1","title":"Design: storage/indexing perf targets, metrics, and budgets","description":"# Task: Design storage/indexing perf targets

## Goal
Define measurable targets and budgets for storage and indexing.

## Requirements
- Define scale targets:
  - panes: N (e.g., 50+)
  - transcript size: M (e.g., multi-GB)
  - ingest rate: bytes/sec
- Define latency budgets:
  - append segment p95
  - FTS query p95 for common queries
  - indexing lag ceiling
- Define health metrics:
  - writer queue depth
  - WAL size / checkpoint cadence
  - index progress per pane

## Acceptance Criteria
- Targets are explicit and can be enforced by benchmarks and/or E2E.
